runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "JavaScript", "build_system": "npm", "package_manager": "npm", "build_commands": ["npm install", "node esbuild.js"], "install_commands": [], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "lts", "system_packages": []}, "repo_type": "cli_tool", "base_image": "node:lts", "base_image_rationale": "Node.js official LTS base image provides a ready-to-use Node/npm environment suitable for building a Node CLI.", "pitfalls": ["package.json does not specify engines; Node version may differ from CI expectations (20.x in workflows).", "esbuild.js targets Node 18 based environments; ensure compatibility with the chosen base image.", "No explicit build artifact beyond build/index.js is produced unless further packaging steps are added (the repo includes additional packaging scripts in CI)."], "notes": "This blueprint assumes building a Node CLI using npm and esbuild as defined by esbuild.js. If you intend to produce distributable binaries (as in CI release steps), extend with packaging steps (pkg or similar) and platform-specific targets."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
# Use base image
FROM node:lts

# Set work directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install dependencies (including dev) to allow building from source
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci || npm install

# Copy the remainder of the source code
COPY . .

# Build the project from source
RUN node esbuild.js

# Optional: prune production dependencies to reduce image size
RUN npm prune --production

# Default command: run the built CLI
CMD ["node", "build/index.js"]

artifacts.content:
# Use base image
FROM node:lts

# Set work directory
WORKDIR /app

# Install dependencies (including dev) to allow building from source
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci || npm install

# Copy the remainder of the source code
COPY . .

# Build the project from source
RUN node esbuild.js

# Optional: prune production dependencies to reduce image size
RUN npm prune --production

# Default command: run the built CLI
CMD ["node", "build/index.js"]
